title: [dragapult] Battle Guide
summary: A physical [dragon]/[ghost] sweeper whose base 142 Speed and [infiltrator] trait crack through screens and substitutes across the whole tier.
strengths: Base 142 Speed is top-tier without a scarf
[dragon-darts] hits both targets in doubles at 75 BP each
weaknesses: Ice, [dragon], [ghost], Dark, and [fairy] all hit for 2x
88/75 physical bulk folds to priority and close-combat

recommendedMoves.reason: Physical [dragon] STAB that hits twice in doubles
[ghost] STAB with a one-turn invulnerability window
Pivot move to keep matchups favorable

recommendedItems.reason: Pushes the 142 Speed tier above every revenge killer
Secures one priority hit so can retaliate

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[dragapult] is the 88/120/75/100/75/142 [dragon]/[ghost] sweeper that defines the top of the Champions MA Speed tier. Both physical and special builds exist, but this guide locks in the [jolly] physical profile because [dragon-darts] and [sucker-punch] are what make it a meta-defining revenge killer.

## Strengths
142 Speed means [dragapult] moves before almost every unboosted attacker in the format, and [infiltrator] strips screens, reflect, and substitute from the equation entirely — a whole archetype of defensive play is just switched off. [dragon-darts] is a two-hit 75 BP STAB that doubles as a spread move for doubles formats, and 120 Attack combined with STAB makes it a one-turn wallbreaker. Access to [sucker-punch] lets [dragapult] finish even scarfed threats that out-speed it.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[dragon]/[ghost] stacks five 2x weaknesses: Ice, [dragon], [ghost], Dark, and [fairy]. The 88/75 physical bulk means ice-shard, [sucker-punch], and mach-punch users revenge kill it at low HP. Kingambit is the obvious counter because it threatens both priority and Dark STAB in one slot, and Steel walls like Gholdengo ignore [dragon-darts] outright.

## Recommended Moveset
[dragon-darts] is locked for STAB. [phantom-force] is the [ghost] STAB of choice despite the one-turn charge because it also avoids strong answers that would otherwise force a switch. [u-turn] is the pivot that keeps [dragapult] healthy and picks better matchups, and [sucker-punch] is the revenge tool against faster scarf users. For a special build, swap [dragon-darts] and [phantom-force] for draco-meteor and shadow-ball.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[choice-scarf] is the primary item when [dragapult]'s role is revenge killing — pushing Speed above any remaining scarfer locks in the role. [focus-sash] fits a [sucker-punch] build that needs one guaranteed priority survival. The ability must be [infiltrator]; clear-body is worse in nearly every matchup. Tera [ghost] boosts [phantom-force] damage, while Tera [fairy] erases Dark and [dragon] weaknesses and gives a clean defensive profile.

## Teammates & Synergy
[dragapult] wants partners that break Steel walls — Fighting, Ground, and Fire wallbreakers all qualify. A [fairy] pivot covers Dark answers in the mirror, and an intimidate support helps cushion the weak physical bulk against priority. Hazard support from stealth-rock amplifies the value of every [u-turn] pivot, because each forced switch chips another mon.

## Play Patterns
Mid-game [u-turn] is the default click — keep recycling entries until the opposing core is chipped, then lock into [phantom-force] plus [dragon-darts] for the sweep. Never click a [dragon] STAB into a known Steel; pivot out instead. Save Tera for the exact turn an Ice or Dark move is telegraphed — that swap usually flips the whole exchange. Below 50% HP, the [sucker-punch] line is the only clean finish.
